Released: 25 December 2024

## Highlights

- *Support for New PG4K Release:* Starting with version 1.1.0, the PGD4K operator now supports the new PG4K Long-Term Support (LTS) release, version 1.25.
- *New PGD Version Support:* PGD version 5.6.1 is now supported.

## Supported versions

- Kubernetes: 1.32, 1.31, 1.30, and 1.29
- PostgreSQL: 17, 16, 15, 14, and 13
- PG4K: v1.22.8, v1.25.0

  Default PGD image: postgresql-pgd 17.2-5.6.1

  Default PGD proxy image: edb-pgd-proxy 5.6.0

  Officially dropped support for PostgreSQL 12

  PostgreSQL 13 support ends on November 12, 2025

This release of EDB PostgresÂ® AI for CloudNativePGâ„˘ Global Cluster includes the following:

## Features

<table class="table w-100"><thead><tr><th>Description</th><th width="10%">Addresses</th></tr></thead><tbody>
<tr><td><details><summary>New Operator Architecture Support</summary><hr/><p>Added support for linux/arm64 and linux/ppc64le architectures.</p></details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Configuration Support for cnpg-i Plugin</summary><hr/><p>Configuration settings for the cnpg-i plugin have been implemented</p></details></td><td>#929</td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Avoid unnecessary failure over</summary><hr/><p>Avoid unnecessary write lead transfer when <code>reconcilePodSpec</code> is used in PG4K
cluster</p></details></td><td>#885</td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Scale Down Write Leader Last</summary><hr/><p>The write leader will be the last PGD node to scale down</p></details></td><td>#920</td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Enhanced Restore Process</summary><hr/><p>The restore process has been improved by splitting it into components, and
using separate jobs for different types of restore and bootstrap operations</p></details></td><td>#817, #852</td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Webhook Validation enhancement</summary><hr/><p>validity of <code>restore.serverNames</code> will be checked via the webhook</p></details></td><td>#932</td></tr>
<tr><td>Include clusterStatus as part of the PGDGroup status for information on the phase of the managed clusters.</td><td>#875</td></tr>
</tbody></table>
